# Parvaz

> A Farsi-first Android **browser tunnel** that routes Chrome / Firefox
> traffic through a user-deployed Google Apps Script relay, using TLS
> SNI concealment and a local on-device MITM to terminate TLS for
> re-encapsulation. Monorepo: Kotlin front end + Go sidecar. Matches
> the architecture of MasterHttpRelayVPN-RUST with different UX.

## Overview

Parvaz ships as one APK. Inside: a Kotlin/Compose UI (Farsi default),
Android `VpnService` subclass, `tun2socks`, and a Go sidecar
(`libparvaz.so`) that runs a local SOCKS5 on `127.0.0.1:1080`. The
sidecar either:
- **SNI-rewrites** Google-owned hosts straight through Google's edge
  (no MITM, no relay), or
- **MITM-intercepts** everything else, reconstructs the HTTP request,
  and sends it through the Apps Script relay wrapped in a JSON envelope
  (`{k, m, u, h, b?, ct?, r}`).

Scope is deliberately browser-only — non-browser Android apps
(Instagram, WhatsApp, banking, streaming) reject user-installed CAs and
will not tunnel through MITM. Documented honestly in README.md and
ARCHITECTURE.md.

## Technology Stack

- Kotlin 2.2.x + Jetpack Compose (Material3), min SDK 24, target SDK 36
- Go 1.24+ (stdlib-first; deps: `github.com/andybalholm/brotli`, `github.com/klauspost/compress/zstd`)
- Android VpnService API + tun2socks
- Gradle 9.3.x (Kotlin DSL) + AGP 9.1.x
- Google Apps Script runtime (user-deployed, free tier, 20K requests/day)
- Light-theme NOTAM / declassified-briefing aesthetic
- Fonts: Vazirmatn (Persian default), Redaction (Latin display), JetBrains Mono

## Architecture

- `app/` — Kotlin + Compose UI (Farsi-first), VpnService, tun2socks, sidecar launcher, MITM CA install flow, NOTAM theme, settings
- `core/fronter/` — TLS-with-custom-SNI dialer + HTTP/1.1 client
- `core/protocol/` — Apps Script JSON envelope encode/decode
- `core/codec/` — gzip / br / zstd / identity decoders
- `core/relay/` — envelope + fronted client + codec glue
- `core/socks5/` — local SOCKS5 listener
- `core/mitm/` — (next milestone) on-device CA + dynamic leaf certs + TLS server
- `core/dispatcher/` — (next milestone) Google-allowlist vs MITM-path decision
- `core/cmd/parvazd/` — sidecar main, packaged per-ABI as `libparvaz.so`
- `reference/` — MasterHttpRelayVPN upstream Python, read-only
- `website/` — bilingual GitHub Pages (EN + FA)

## Key Files

- `CLAUDE.md` — architecture, layer rules, required skills, engineering principles
- `PLAN.md` — TDD milestone list
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — full data path diagram and core/app boundary
- `core/go.mod` — Go module `github.com/cocodedk/parvaz/core`
- `apps_script/Code.gs` — the server Parvaz talks to (deploy to your own Google account)
- `version.txt` — semantic version

## Wire Protocol

```
Per tunneled HTTP request:
    TCP connect <google_ip>:443  (default 216.239.38.120)
    TLS handshake  SNI = www.google.com  (DPI-visible)
    POST /macros/s/<DEPLOYMENT_ID>/exec HTTP/1.1
    Host: script.google.com
    Content-Type: application/json
    body = { k, m, u, h, b?, ct?, r }

Response = { s, h, b } or { e: "..." }.

For *.google.com / *.youtube.com / etc.: skip the envelope. Direct TCP
to google_ip with SNI=www.google.com, Host=<target> — Google's edge
routes by Host. No Apps Script quota consumed, no MITM needed.
```

## Access URL format

`parvaz://<deployment-id>/<access-key>#<optional-display-name>`

Registered URI scheme — Telegram/WhatsApp deep link → opens Parvaz
directly. Same format encoded in QR codes.

## Links

- Repo: https://github.com/cocodedk/parvaz
- Releases: https://github.com/cocodedk/parvaz/releases/latest

- Built by: [Cocode](https://cocode.dk) · [Babak Bandpey](https://linkedin.com/in/babakbandpey)

## Legal / ToS

Google Apps Script's terms forbid this use. Deploy `Code.gs` to your
own Google account only. Personal, research, and educational use only.
